programmes.show_description: (1964) Romcom starring Rock Hudson and Doris Day. Thinking he has just a few months to live, a man makes his wife suspect he's having an affair when he tries to find her a new partner.
(1958) Western. Van Heflin stars as a veteran frontiersman whose determination to mould his sons in his gun-totin' image backfires disastrously. With Tab Hunter and James Darren.
(1970) Multi-Oscar-winning, fact-based, Second World War drama starring George C Scott, who gives a vivid portrait of the famously straight-talking US commander General George Patton.
(2015) Tom Hanks and Mark Rylance star in Steven Spielberg's Cold War spy drama. After gaining notoriety for defending a Soviet spy, a US lawyer then tries to free a CIA pilot.
(2023) Tina Satter's tense fact-based whistleblower drama stars Sydney Sweeney (White Lotus) as Reality Winner - a young woman being questioned by the FBI about leaked top-secret documents.
(2010) Action-thriller starring Angelina Jolie. A CIA agent goes on the run after a Russian defector claims that she's a sleeper agent who's about to commit a terrible act of terrorism.
(2020) Dark comedy drama based on a viral Twitter thread. Dancer Zola (Taylour Paige) joins a road trip to Tampa, where she gets dragged too far into the sex work world. Adults only.
(2020) Comedy-drama. A college student struggles to keep a lid on her secrets as she awkwardly encounters her sugar daddy and the ex-girlfriend she's still hung up on at a shiva.
(1959) Western. Randolph Scott plays a bounty hunter who has unfinished business with the brother of his latest target. But his quest for revenge looks like it could be undermined.
(1949) Western. Gregory Peck plays the leader of an outlaw gang whose plans to rob an old man and his granddaughter of their gold go seriously awry. With Anne Baxter and Richard Widmark.
(1943) Drama. Director Sam Wood's epic adaptation of Ernest Hemingway's classic story about the Spanish Civil War. Starring Oscar-nominees Gary Cooper and Ingrid Bergman.
(1952) Screwball comedy starring Cary Grant as a scientist who drinks the elixir of youth and starts acting the fool, as does his strait-laced wife (Ginger Rogers).
(2004) All-action thriller. Queen Latifah stars as the driver of the fastest taxi in New York, who teams up with an inept cop (Jimmy Fallon) to catch a team of female bank-robbers.
(2014) Thriller. Denzel Washington stars as an ex-soldier who comes out of retirement to help a teenage sex worker who's been assaulted by her pimp.
(2006) Pulsating sci-fi action with Denzel Washington and Val Kilmer. An AFT agent uses new technology to reach back in time, stop a ferry bombing, and save the love of his life. Simple.
